Notability.Summary: Harvard–MIT geneticist who helped initiate the Human Genome Project and founded the Personal Genome Project, with pioneering work in DNA sequencing, gene editing and synthetic biology.

Sources.Title: George Church — Harvard Personal Genome Project public profile (hu43860C)
Harvard Wyss Institute — George Church biography

TranscriptionNotes: The Harvard Personal Genome Project profile identifies George M. Church and labels Health records 2010 as a participant upload; the linked Wyss Institute biography identifies him as George Church, Ph.D. Both source-backed variants are recorded as aliases for collision protection. The uploaded clinical note identifies him as 56 years old; no exact date of birth is stored here.

The signed 1 November 2010 annual-physical note presents the comprehensive metabolic panel, cardio CRP and CBC together. The CBC is marked as ordered for 28 October, but the source does not print one specimen-collection date for every component. Date therefore stores the published report date with DateBasis='Report'; it is not presented as a draw date.
Albumin was converted from g/dL to g/L by multiplying by 10.
Creatinine was converted from mg/dL to µmol/L by multiplying by 88.4.
Glucose was converted from mg/dL to mmol/L by multiplying by 0.0555.
